steps.title: Recognize the graph as the mass constraint equation
steps.content:
The line represents all valid combinations of M-type and K-type stars. Let $m$ = mass of each M-type star and $k$ = mass of each K-type star. The total mass constraint is:
$$mx + ky = 132,778$$
Reading from the graph: x-intercept at $(160, 0)$ and y-intercept at $(0, 140)$.

steps.title: Find the mass per M-type star using the x-intercept
steps.content: At the $x$-intercept, all $132{,}778$ quettagrams come from M-type stars only because $y=0$. If each M-type star has mass $m$, then $$160m=132{,}778.$$ So $$m=\frac{132{,}778}{160}=829.8625.$$

steps.title: Confirm the choice
steps.content: The answer choices are estimates based on reading the graph, so use the closest option. $829.8625$ is closest to $828$, so choice A is correct.
